macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 and Dow Decline Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict Despite Trump Comments
items.summary: U.S. stock indexes are mostly lower with the S&P 500 down 0.15% (10 points), Dow down 0.15% (73 points), and Nasdaq down 0.05% (14 points), even as oil prices sink to $88.75 following President Trump's forecast of an end to the Iran conflict and consideration of taking over the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: U.S. Stocks Struggle Higher as Iran Conflict Persists
items.summary: Nasdaq edges up 0.03% while S&P 500 falls 0.18%, Dow drops 0.13%, and Russell 2000 declines 0.28%, amid continued Iran tensions despite positive Trump remarks.

items.title: Bank of America Reiterates Buy on Nvidia Ahead of GTC Conference
items.summary: Bank of America maintains a buy rating on Nvidia, expressing bullishness heading into the company's GTC conference on March 16.

items.title: Wolfe Research Raises Amazon Price Target to $255
items.summary: Wolfe analysts reiterate outperform on Amazon, lifting the price target from $250 to $255, citing accelerating AWS growth, strong retail, and margin expansion.

items.title: B. Riley Initiates Buy on Strategy (MSTR) for Bitcoin Exposure
items.summary: B. Riley starts coverage with a buy on Strategy, highlighting attractive dividends and long-term appreciation from Bitcoin holdings demonstrated over six years.

items.title: Gold Surges and Bitcoin Rallies Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Gold rises $121.35 to $5,218 while Bitcoin climbs $2,315 to $70,758 as stock markets dip.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: MicroStrategy Purchases 17,994 Bitcoins for $1.28 Billion
items.summary: MicroStrategy, dubbed a Bitcoin Treasury Company, acquired 17,994 BTC between March 2nd and 8th at roughly $70,946 per bitcoin, causing its stock (MSTR) to jump 3.06%. This reinforces Bitcoin's scarcity narrative as the network nears 20 million coins mined.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Analysis Shows Swing Trade Range and Upward Momentum
items.summary: Bitcoin is in a key swing trade range with support and resistance levels highlighted, up 3.7% recently amid expectations of an aggressive March rally. Analysts note Bitcoin breaking out of ranging market, with dominance analysis suggesting strength.

items.title: Oil Prices Plunge 32% Impacting Crypto Markets
items.summary: Oil prices collapsed 32% following U.S. considerations on Hormuz Strait control and strikes on Iran's oil facilities, influencing global markets and crypto sentiment. Recession fears are rising alongside this volatility.
items.source: TitoVlogs and Recession Fears Crypto Update

items.title: Coinbase CEO Discusses AI Agents Driving Crypto Growth
items.summary: Coinbase CEO highlights how AI agents could massively boost crypto payments and digital asset adoption. This ties into broader AI and cryptocurrency integration trends.

items.title: Ethereum Staking Demand Surges with Validator Queue Vertical
items.summary: Ethereum validator queue is surging vertically, increasing staking demand amid network effects, credible neutrality, and DeFi collateral use. Analysts predict potential rise to $80k.

items.title: Altcoins Like Solana, Chainlink Show Strength Amid Market Recovery
items.summary: Solana RWA developments, Chainlink (LINK) investment opportunities, and other altcoins like AVAX, XRP gaining traction as Bitcoin leads out of range. Partnerships for RWA tokenization accelerating in Europe.
items.source: Crypto Banter and Market Update Streams

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Sydney Researchers Develop Ultra-Compact Nanophotonic AI Chip Operating at Speed of Light
items.summary: University of Sydney researchers created a prototype nanophotonic AI chip that performs neural network calculations using light, achieving 90-99% accuracy on classifying over 10,000 biomedical MRI images with lower heat and energy use than electronic chips.

items.title: Berkeley Lab Unveils AQuaRef: AI Tool Cracks Hard-to-Map Protein Structures
items.summary: Scientists at Berkeley Lab developed AQuaRef, combining AI and quantum calculations to refine protein structures more accurately and cheaply, successfully mapping the elusive DJ-1 protein linked to Parkinson's disease.

items.title: AFEELA Advances AI-Based Trajectory Inference for Autonomous Driving
items.summary: AFEELA announced improvements in AI trajectory inference for self-driving cars, using SOTA tech, simulations, and MLOps to handle complex traffic, reduce development time by 90%, and prevent performance degradation.

items.title: UCSD Introduces Zephyrus: AI Agent for Weather and Climate Analysis
items.summary: UC San Diego researchers launched Zephyrus, an AI agent that queries weather data in natural language, translates to code, and responds in plain English, aiming to democratize earth science research.

items.title: Multi-Agent AI Systems Outperform Single Agents in Healthcare Applications
items.summary: New research shows orchestrated multi-agent AI systems surpass single-agent models in healthcare tasks like record management and medication assistance.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Multiplayer Shooter Highguard to Shut Down After Failing to Retain Players
items.summary: Developer Wildlight Entertainment announced the permanent shutdown of Highguard on March 12, just weeks after its January launch, highlighting challenges in the competitive gaming market with player counts dropping from 100,000 to around 500 daily.

items.title: GTA 6 Clears Holiday 2026 Release Calendar as Publishers Avoid Clash
items.summary: Grand Theft Auto 6's November 19 release has left October through December empty of major AAA titles, with games like Marvel's Wolverine and NBA 2K27 shifting to earlier in the year to avoid competition.

items.title: Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection Set for March 16 Multi-Platform Launch
items.summary: Capcom's latest Monster Hunter spin-off launches March 16, 2026 on Switch 2, PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC, praised in early impressions as the series' most mature entry after 45 hours of play.

items.title: ZeniMax Online Studios Reportedly Developing Unannounced Game
items.summary: ZeniMax Online Studios, known for Elder Scrolls Online, is working on a new unannounced project, as reported on March 10.

items.title: VANISHED: Puzzle Quest to Launch Educating Players on Muslim Golden Age
items.summary: Unity Productions Foundation's VANISHED: Puzzle Quest, highlighting scientific achievements of the Muslim Golden Age, is set for global release on March 20, 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Volkswagen Slashes 50,000 Jobs After Profits Collapse by Nearly Half
items.summary: Volkswagen announced major layoffs of 50,000 jobs following a sharp decline in profits. The decision comes amid economic pressures and industry challenges.

items.title: New FDA-Approved Drug Helps Patients Stay on Ozempic and Wegovy Longer
items.summary: The FDA approved a new drug from Vanda Pharmaceuticals to assist patients in maintaining treatment with weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy. The approval was highlighted alongside a legal win for jet lag treatment.
